successCriteria.description: src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditRecord with fields id, entityType, entityId, action and CreateAuditRecordInput with fields entityType, entityId, action.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports AuditRepository with create and findByEntity methods using AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput types from audit.model.ts.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exports an abstract PostgreSqlAuditRepository class implementing the AuditRepository contract with matching method signatures.
Repository contracts use AuditRecord types imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts and introduce no circular dependencies.
tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.repository.spec.ts exists, uses Vitest, and verifies audit module contract exports and TypeScript type compatibility.
The canonical audit_records schema is represented by the contract design with columns id, entity_type, entity_id, and action matching the provided schema definition.

constraints: Keep scope limited to audit persistence contracts and related type definitions/tests only.
Repository pattern: all database access must be expressed through repository interfaces/contracts; no direct database access implementation in this phase.
Audit support must remain isolated and reusable by later workflow services.
Use the exact interfaces and method signatures specified in the architecture definition.
All repository method signatures must use AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts.
Avoid circular dependencies between audit model and repository contracts.
Use TypeScript, Node.js 20, npm, and Vitest conventions already established in the project.
Audit records are a project-wide requirement for future state-changing operations, but this phase defines only the audit domain contracts, not behavior.
Error handling principle applies to future implementations: no unhandled promise rejections in async code.
No sensitive data in logs; this phase should not introduce logging.
RBAC and input validation are API-boundary concerns and should not be implemented in these contract files.
outOfScope: Implementing approval or rejection workflow services.
Creating Fastify routes, controllers, or API endpoints.
Audit service logic or audit record creation workflows.
PostgreSQL repository implementation details beyond abstract contracts.
Database migrations or schema deployment artifacts.
Employee, LeavePolicy, LeaveBalance, LeaveRequest, or Notification domain models and repositories.
Any files outside src/modules/audit/* and tests/unit/modules/audit/* required for the specified contracts and tests.
